seo.title: Mortgages for Truck Drivers | Non-Traditional Income | Green Street Capital
seo.description: Mortgage solutions for truck drivers and owner-operators. Flexible documentation for non-traditional income. NY, NJ, FL, PA. NMLS #2066586.

hero.heading: Mortgages for Truck Drivers
hero.subheading: Specialized mortgage solutions for professional truck drivers with non-traditional income.

intro.heading: Understanding Truck Driver Income
paragraphs: Traditional lenders often struggle with truck driver income structures. We understand the trucking industry and work with lenders who appreciate your unique financial situation.
Whether you are an owner-operator or company driver, we review how you are paid and which documentation best supports a strong application.
bullets: Owner-operators with variable income based on miles and rates
Company drivers with per diem and bonus structures
Team drivers with split income arrangements
Drivers with significant business expenses that reduce taxable income

sidebar.heading: Why Traditional Lenders Struggle
sidebar.body: W-2 and tax-return-only underwriting may understate earning power when expenses, per diem, or seasonal miles are involved.

programs.title: Bank Statement Loans
programs.description: Use business or personal bank deposits to help document cash flow.

programs.title: Non-QM Options
programs.description: Alternatives when standard QM documentation is not the best fit.

programs.title: Conventional / FHA
programs.description: Traditional paths when income can be fully documented.

programs.title: Purchase & Refinance
programs.description: Buy a home or refinance an existing mortgage when eligible.

faqs.question: Can truck drivers get a mortgage with variable income?
faqs.answer: Often yes. Lenders may average income over time or use alternative documentation depending on the program.

faqs.question: Do you work with owner-operators?
faqs.answer: Yes. We regularly review owner-operator scenarios and available program options.

faqs.question: What should I prepare?
faqs.answer: Recent bank statements, tax returns if available, CDL/employment details, and a list of debts and assets help us move faster.

cta.heading: Ready to explore financing?
cta.body: Talk with a loan officer who understands non-traditional trucking income.
